findings.description: flush() opens and tears down a fresh SMTP connection (TCP + EHLO + likely STARTTLS on port 587) for every message inside the loop, turning O(1) connection setup into O(N) round-trips that dominate send cost; connect once before the loop and reuse the server.
smtplib.SMTP() is constructed with no timeout, so an unresponsive SMTP host makes flush() block the entire pipeline indefinitely (latency/availability risk on the connection path).
'field' is imported from dataclasses but never used (dead import).
Dedup key is msg.to only, so two distinct messages to the same recipient are silently skipped; not a perf issue, deferred to critic-logic.
